Engineers

BUSINESS
Efficient distributed team management at iDrift AS
Easy communication for 1000 agents at GUT contact
Managing hundreds of projects at End Point Dev
Worldwide operations and AI at WindBorne
More efficient communication than Slack at Semsee
Open distributed communication at Atolio
EDUCATION and RESEARCH
Organized chat for 1000s of students at TUM
Communication hub across 6 continents at UCSD
Connecting across generations at the National University of Córdoba
Research collaboration at scale in the Lean mathematical community
OPEN SOURCE and COMMUNITIES
Inclusive discussion in the open-source Asciidoctor community
Faster decision-making in the Rust language community
Platform for a worldwide community since 2013 at Recurse Center
Professional community support at Rush Stack
Collaboration drives innovation at Mixxx

GitHub
Pricing
Download
New organization
Log in
Organized chat for distributed teams
See how we use Zulip
No account needed
Create an organization
Anonymously if you like
Contact sales
Talk to a helpful human
Designed for async conversations.
Here's what it looks like in action.
Get an overview of conversations with unread messages in your inbox.
Focus on one conversation at a time, no matter how many others are happening.
Reply to each conversation in context.
Conversations continue seamlessly over time.
Start a new conversation by giving it a brief topic.
Zulip empowers remote and flexible work.
Check out these case studies to see the impact.
Work better together
The first-class threads in Zulip are
absolutely critical to how we work
. So many people on Hacker News talk about using Zulip — I'm so glad we joined them!
David Lanstein
,
co-founder and CEO of Atolio
Empower leaders
Using Zulip significantly
increases the size of the team
for which a manager can meaningfully know what’s going on.
Gaute Lund
,
co-founder of iDrift AS
Make decisions faster
Some decisions that were blocked for months on
                                GitHub were
resolved within 24 hours
on Zulip.
Josh Triplett
,
Rust Language team co-lead
Simplify knowledge management
Using Zulip in a way that feels natural creates
                                an
organized repository
of knowledge
as a
                                side effect
.
Max McCrea
,
Co-founder of Monadical
Organize 100s of workstreams
Other apps like Slack would struggle with
                                organizing the flow of information in a complex
                                organization like ours. Zulip’s UI makes it easy
                                to
access all the information you need
.
Jon Jensen
,
CTO of End Point
                                Dev
Communicate with efficiency
I don’t like going back to Slack now. It’s just
                                not as efficient a way to
organize
                                communication
.
James van Lommel
,
Director of Engineering at Semsee
You own your data
Escape corporate vendor lock-in. You can
self-host
Zulip’s 100%
open-source
software for full data sovereignty, or start
                            with a convenient
cloud
                            solution
and
move
any time.
We make it easy to switch from
Slack
,
Teams
and
other
                            tools
.
Curious to learn more?
Dive into our detailed guide for organizations like yours.
Business
Organizations from small businesses to enterprises communicate more efficiently.
Research
For your group, lab, department or scientific field.
Education
Communication hub for classes, in-person or online.
Open-source
Grow and engage your community.
Non-profits, Governments
Free or highly-discounted plans are available for most non-business uses.
Events and conferences
For organizers and attendees at your conference, workshop, or hackathon.

On this page
Overview

Mastering Zulip
Other resources
Zulip help center
Welcome to the help center for
Zulip organized team chat
!
Here, you’ll find up-to-date information on how to use Zulip, or administer a
Zulip organization. These pages are designed to guide you as you learn your way
around Zulip. They’ll also be your go-to if you’re ever unsure how something
works, or if you need to explain it to someone else.
Guides for new users
Section titled “Guides for new users”
The following guides will help you get started, and more can be found in the
left sidebar.
Getting started with Zulip
Choosing a team chat app
Why Zulip
Trying out Zulip
Zulip Cloud or self-hosting?
Moving to Zulip
Mastering Zulip
Section titled “Mastering Zulip”
If you’re using Zulip a lot, it’s worthwhile to learn workflows that will help you
spend that time efficiently. Check out:
Keyboard shortcuts
: Mastering a few keyboard
shortcuts (like
N
to go to the next unread topic) can speed up your
workflows.
Reading strategies
: Learn different ways to
find what you want to read.
Mastering the compose box
: Learn about
everything Zulip’s compose box lets you do.
Message formatting
: Learn about
quotes, spoilers, global times, and more.
Search filters
: Learn how to find
messages based on where they were sent, whether they contain links or
attachments, and other details.
Other resources
Section titled “Other resources”
You may also want to check out:
An overview
of the features available in Zulip.
Installation
instructions
for setting up a self-hosted Zulip server, and documentation on
running Zulip
in production
.
API documentation
for writing integrations or bots
using the Zulip API.
Contributing
guide
,
with step-by-step instructions on how to get started contributing code to
Zulip.
If you have any questions that aren’t answered here,
let us
know
!
We work hard to make Zulip’s help center comprehensive and easy to follow.
Please drop by the
#documentation
channel in the
Zulip development
community
to share ideas for how to
make it better.
Your feedback helps us make Zulip better for everyone! Please
contact us
with questions, suggestions, and feature requests.

hits.title: Zulip 4.0: Threaded open source team chat
Zulip 1.8: Free software Slack alternative with email-style threading
Zulip 3.0: Threaded Open Source Team Chat
Zulip Server 1.9: HipChat import and much more

analysis.name: Zulip
analysis.value_proposition: Open-source, threaded team chat designed for async conversations with topic-based organization, available self-hosted or as managed cloud.
analysis.ideal_customer: Distributed teams, open-source communities, research groups, and organizations that need organized async communication across many workstreams

features.name: topics
features.description: Brief topic labels that organize conversations within channels for threaded discussion
features.evidence_quote: Start a new conversation by giving it a brief topic.

features.name: channels
features.description: Public and private channels for organizing team communication
features.evidence_quote: Introduction to channels / Create a channel / Private channels / Public channels

features.name: inbox view
features.description: Overview of conversations with unread messages
features.evidence_quote: Get an overview of conversations with unread messages in your inbox.

features.name: threaded conversations
features.evidence_quote: The first-class threads in Zulip are absolutely critical to how we work

features.name: user groups
features.description: Custom user groups for permissions and mentions
features.evidence_quote: Create user groups / Manage user groups

features.name: sso
features.description: SAML-based single sign-on with Okta, OneLogin, Microsoft Entra ID

features.name: ad/ldap sync
features.description: Active Directory / LDAP user and group sync

features.name: data import
features.description: Import from Slack, Teams, Discord, Mattermost, Rocket.Chat
features.evidence_quote: Import from Mattermost / Microsoft Teams / Slack / Rocket.Chat

features.name: self-hosting
features.description: 100% open-source software for full data sovereignty
features.evidence_quote: self-host Zulip's 100% open-source software for full data sovereignty

features.name: file storage
features.description: File uploads and storage (5-25 GB per user)
features.evidence_quote: File storage up to 25 GB per user

features.name: search history
features.description: Unlimited message search history on paid plans

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native desktop apps for Mac, Windows, Linux

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android mobile apps

features.name: mobile push notifications
features.description: Unlimited mobile push notifications on paid self-hosted plans

features.name: incoming webhooks
features.description: Incoming webhook support for custom integrations

features.name: zapier integration
features.evidence_quote: And hundreds more through Zapier and IFTTT.

features.name: ifttt integration
features.evidence_quote: And hundreds more through Zapier and IFTTT.

features.name: custom emoji
features.description: Upload custom emoji for the organization

features.name: read receipts
features.description: See who has read a message

features.name: email to channel
features.description: Post to a channel via email

features.name: move messages
features.description: Move messages to another topic or channel

features.name: keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Extensive keyboard shortcuts for power users

features.name: custom profile fields
features.description: Custom profile fields for organization members

features.name: video calls
features.description: Video call integration via Jitsi, BigBlueButton, Zoom
features.evidence_quote: Start a call / Configure call provider

features.name: organization import
features.description: Import full organizations from other chat tools

features.name: demo organizations
features.description: Spin up demo organizations without an account
features.evidence_quote: No account needed / Create an organization / Anonymously if you like

features.name: channel posting policy
features.description: Configure who can post in channels

differentiators: Fully owned by a nonprofit (Zulip Foundation)
Free Standard plan for open-source projects, research, education, and non-profits
Move freely between Zulip Cloud and self-hosting with export/import tools
Designed for async conversations at scale — 100s of workstreams

recent_ships.what: Zulip fully owned by nonprofit Zulip Foundation
recent_ships.source: Announcing the Zulip Foundation! Zulip is now fully owned by a nonprofit.

praised: 'The first-class threads in Zulip are absolutely critical to how we work' — David Lanstein, Atolio
'Using Zulip significantly increases the size of the team for which a manager can meaningfully know what's going on' — Gaute Lund, iDrift AS
'Some decisions that were blocked for months on GitHub were resolved within 24 hours on Zulip' — Josh Triplett, Rust
'creates an organized repository of knowledge as a side effect' — Max McCrea, Monadical
Repeated HN interest in threading model as Slack alternative

free_tier.limits: 10,000 messages of search history; 5 GB total file storage; mobile notifications limited to 10 users for non-eligible orgs on self-hosted

tiers.name: Cloud Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 10k message search history, 5GB storage, full team chat, guests, integrations

tiers.name: Cloud Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited history, 5GB/user, custom groups, retention, branding, priority support

tiers.name: Cloud Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: 25GB/user, SAML SSO, SCIM, custom domain, guest access limits (10 user min)

tiers.name: Self-hosted Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Zulip features; mobile notifications up to 10 users (or unlimited for eligible communities)

tiers.name: Self-hosted Basic
tiers.headline_inclusions: All features, unlimited mobile notifications, 85%+ discount for non-workplace users

tiers.name: Self-hosted Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: SAML SSO, Entra ID, AD/LDAP sync, guests, compliance exports (25 user min)

tiers.name: Self-hosted Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: OIDC SSO, SAML/LDAP group sync, SCIM, custom dev, implementation consulting, phone support

pricing.notes: Plus tier requires 10 user minimum; Self-hosted Business requires 25 user minimum. $20/month off first year on self-hosted Basic and Business. 85%+ discount on self-hosted Basic for non-workplace users. Free Standard plan available for open-source projects, academic research, non-profits, and communities. Sponsors over 1500 organizations.

recent_hn.title: Zulip 12.0: Organized chat for distributed teams
Show HN: Moomacha – Zulip Native Agents Orchestrator
RustChat is a minimal team messenger, alternative to Slack, Mattermost, Zulip
